lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- Answering Questions About Your Work -- Clarify, Paraphrase, Buy Time
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 7 -- Answering Questions About Your Work

listenings.text: Answering tough questions is a skill, and like any skill, you can learn it. Here are my three best tips. First, stay calm and buy yourself a few seconds. A simple opener like, that is a great question, gives your brain time to organize the answer. Second, be honest. If you do not know something, never guess a number. Just say you will get back to the person with the exact figure. Honesty builds trust far more than a wrong answer. And third, when your answer is complex, paraphrase it. Use a phrase like, in other words, or, to put it simply, and then give a short, clear version. Remember: a calm, honest, simple answer is always stronger than a fast, nervous one. Take your time, and you will do great.
